description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the image registry.gitlab.com/buildstream/buildstream-docker-images/testsuite-debian:10-${DOCKER_IMAGE_VERSION} with the docker:stable-dind service, defining variables CI_PROJECT_REPOSITORY_LANGUAGES=python, DOCKER_IMAGE_VERSION, INTEGRATION_CACHE and a cache keyed by $CI_JOB_NAME- storing the cache/ directory. In the test stage add jobs tests-centos-7.7, tests-debian-10, tests-fedora-31, tests-fedora-32, tests-fedora-33, tests-fedora-missing-deps, tests-fedora-update-deps, tests-no-usedevelop, tests-plugins-master, tests-remote-cache, tests-remote-execution, tests-ubuntu-18.04, tests-userchroot, tests-bb-remote-cache, overnight-tests, overnight-tests-no-cache, overnight-randomized, lint, mypy, docs, each with before_script "mount", "df -h", "tox --version" (or additional steps like dnf install, pip3 install, git clone, make) then creating "${INTEGRITY_CACHE}" directory, adding a buildstream user, chowning the workspace, and running "su buildstream -c \"${TEST_COMMAND}\"" (or specific tox commands such as "tox -e format-check,lint", "tox -e mypy", "tox -e docs", "tox -e py36-randomized,py37-randomized,py38-randomized-nocover -- --color=yes --integration"), using OS‑specific images, setting job‑specific variables (e.g., PYTEST_ARGS, BST_EXT_REF, FD_SDK_REF, ARTIFACT_CACHE_SERVICE), publishing .coverage-reports as artifacts, and for remote‑cache jobs adding after_script to stop, log, and bring down docker‑compose; these jobs run on all branches except schedules, with overnight jobs limited to schedules only. In the post stage add a coverage job that copies .coverage-reports to coverage-sources, runs "tox -e coverage", copies reports to coverage-report, and defines a coverage regex, and an analysis job that installs radon and flake8_polyfill then generates maintainability index, cyclomatic complexity and raw metrics JSON files under analysis/. Both jobs have artifacts paths and run except schedules. In the publish stage add a pages job that runs only on the master branch, creates the ACME challenge file in public/.well-known/acme-challenge, copies the coverage-report into public/coverage, and publishes the public/ directory as a pages artifact, with dependencies on coverage and docs.
